title: Writing – Part 1: Essay
description: Your class has attended a panel discussion on work culture reform. Write an essay for your tutor, discussing two of the points in your notes and explaining which you think is more important, giving reasons for your opinion.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the methods in your notes. You should explain which method is more important, giving reasons for your opinion. You may, if you wish, make use of the opinions expressed during the discussion, but you should use your own words as far as possible.

methods: Flexible working hours to improve work-life balance
Strict performance-based evaluations to enhance productivity
Increased remote work opportunities to attract diverse talent
discussionOpinions: I believe flexible hours reduce stress significantly.
Remote work opens doors to talented individuals globally.

assessment.content: Discusses exactly 2 of the 3 methods; argues for one being more important.
assessment.organisation: Clear intro, 2 body paragraphs, conclusion; logical connectives.
assessment.language: C1 range: complex sentences, hedging language, academic vocabulary.

content.modelAnswer: In modern work culture, reform is essential to address evolving employee needs and business efficiency. This essay will discuss flexible working hours and strict performance-based evaluations, ultimately favoring the former. Flexible working hours offer employees the ability to manage their time, potentially reducing stress and increasing job satisfaction. For instance, companies like Google have reported increased employee happiness after implementing such measures. While it is true that performance evaluations aim to boost productivity by holding individuals accountable, they can sometimes lead to a stressful environment that stifles creativity. On the other hand, flexible working hours encourage a focus on output rather than clock-watching, fostering a more innovative atmosphere. Perhaps the most significant factor is the personal autonomy that flexible hours provide, which is crucial for maintaining a motivated workforce. Ultimately, although both approaches have merit, flexible working hours appear to offer a more sustainable long-term advantage by aligning work commitments with personal well-being.
